records.construction: The committed manifest uses deterministic no-toolchain verdicts; live compiler replay separately ensures divergent controls do not degrade to safe or uncovered verdicts.
limitations: Small by design; it stress-tests boundaries rather than estimating population rates.
The byte-stable manifest intentionally excludes host-dependent live replay output.
records.provenance: Hand-crafted boundary cases from internal red-team pressure points: divergent controls, safe one-edit twins, and loud abstentions.
records.real_code_evidence: When clang/UBSan and a target compiler are available, divergent controls are replayed and must remain flagged.
records.title: Hand-crafted adversarial near-miss corpus

records.construction: Every responsible finding is mirrored into a regression entry with source hashes, witness input, safe input, and a reproduction bundle.
limitations: The corpus size follows the number of responsibly recorded findings.
It inherits the upstream-claim restrictions from the findings ledger.
records.provenance: Frozen regressions are confirmed extraction-unit divergences derived from the responsible findings lane; they are not upstream CVEs or repository-defect claims without a separate upstream source audit.
records.real_code_evidence: The gate lints bundles, checks frozen source hashes, and replays the live witness when clang/UBSan plus the target compiler are available.
records.title: Frozen regression corpus from caught divergences

records.construction: Checked-in C extraction units are regenerated into Rust with c2rust; the verifier records symbolic verdicts and source/target hashes.
limitations: Extraction units are minimized families, not vendored full upstream trees.
Compiler-backed regeneration requires a local c2rust binary.
records.provenance: Twelve real-library extraction families are transpiled by the actual c2rust C2Rust 0.22.1 binary.
records.real_code_evidence: Focused tests compile every C source and generated Rust artifact; when c2rust is installed, checked-in Rust is byte-compared to fresh output.
records.title: Tier-1 c2rust-output extraction corpus

records.construction: The zoo indexes divergent exhibits by class and pair and regenerates docs/zoo.md from corpus data.
limitations: The zoo is a curated reference index, not a statistically sampled benchmark.
Live reconfirmation is consistency-only when no target toolchain is present.
records.provenance: Generated from the live idiomatic and multi-pair corpora, preserving each exhibit's source, translation, witness, safe input, and provenance.
records.real_code_evidence: confirm_zoo re-runs every available divergent witness and safe input through the real compiler-backed harness.
records.title: Machine-readable divergence zoo

records.construction: Each subject is wrapped into runnable C/Rust programs and evaluated against SemRec, c2rust-style tests, optional Miri, and equal-budget fuzzing.
limitations: Miri is Rust-only and is recorded as structural applicability, not as a cross-language oracle.
This benchmark reuses c2rust subjects and should not be counted as an independent corpus.
records.provenance: Benchmark subjects are the checked Tier-1 c2rust-output corpus; this record is labeled as a benchmark to avoid double-counting the c2rust population.
records.real_code_evidence: The focused gate confirms SemRec on the real c2rust subjects and runs the baseline checks with the same corpus inputs when toolchains exist.
records.title: Same-corpus existing-tools benchmark
records.unit: c2rust subject reused for baseline comparison

records.construction: Repository candidates are scored deterministically; checked-in extraction samples are verified through the same symbolic verdict layer.
limitations: Samples are extraction units derived from port families, not whole-repo audits.
The live GitHub search path is optional and not part of byte-stable CI.
records.provenance: Seeded and optionally live-ranked public C-to-Rust port families including coreutils, sudo-rs, and zlib-rs.
records.real_code_evidence: The checked samples compile as real C/Rust code when toolchains are available; the divergent sudo/coreutils-style samples are compiler-confirmed through the verifier lane.
records.title: GitHub-mined active C-to-Rust port samples

records.construction: Each historical CVE entry points to a replay template, NVD CWE query, per-language target source, witness inputs, and safe controls.
limitations: The corpus reproduces weakness classes associated with CVEs; it is not original vulnerable vendor source or vendored third-party source.
records.provenance: CVE IDs and NVD CWE families are used as weakness-class provenance; replays are minimized examples, not original vulnerable vendor source.
records.real_code_evidence: Representative templates and bundles are compiled and replayed against real clang/UBSan plus rustc/go when those toolchains are available.
records.title: Historical CVE weakness-class replay corpus

records.construction: Each item carries C source, idiomatic targets, a UB-triggering input, and a safe input; Step 161 expands the real-port family coverage.
limitations: Functions are extraction units, not whole applications.
The corpus emphasizes integer/runtime-contract divergences and true controls.
records.provenance: Real-world-shaped human ports, including coreutils/sudo-rs/zlib-rs classes, with non-literal Rust and Go translations.
records.real_code_evidence: Focused tests compile and replay Step 161 items through real clang/UBSan, rustc, and go when available.
records.title: Human-idiomatic port corpus

records.construction: The full corpus census counts exact LOC, distinct-program hashes, label balance, pair balance, and class balance; live runs execute a deterministic seeded sample.
limitations: The scale population is generated rather than mined from third-party repositories.
CI normally samples the population instead of compiling every item.
records.provenance: Mechanically generated, distinct, compilable C-to-Rust/C-to-Go programs over catalogued divergence and safe-control families.
records.real_code_evidence: The live sample is labeled by real clang/UBSan plus rustc/go when available, with a reproducible verdict-layer hash for a fixed seed.
records.title: 1M-LOC generated migration stress corpus

records.construction: Deterministic fixtures retain prompt/model provenance and are labeled only through real clang/UBSan plus rustc execution in the study gate.
limitations: The translations are frozen fixtures, not an online model benchmark.
Reported live metrics depend on local compiler availability.
records.provenance: Frozen GitHub Copilot CLI/GPT-5.5 translations derived from the Tier-1 c2rust real-library extraction families.
records.real_code_evidence: The focused gate checks corpus size, hash stability, Wilson intervals, and a seeded live real-compiler sample.
records.title: Frozen LLM-transpiler scale corpus

records.construction: Divergent functions must flag on every available target; equivalent functions must stay silent across all target pairs.
limitations: Swift coverage is opportunistic and depends on local toolchain availability.
The corpus is a focused generality check rather than a large-scale scrape.
records.provenance: C functions translated into Rust, Go, and Swift in deliberately varied transpiler/LLM-style forms to test cross-pair invariance.
records.real_code_evidence: Live confirmation compiles and runs clang/UBSan plus every available target compiler and requires a stable verdict hash across runs.
records.title: Tier-3 multi-pair translation corpus

records.construction: All items are covered by registered oracles, range-pruned, and checked against bounded proof inputs to bound false-positive behavior.
limitations: The corpus bounds false positives over its generated safe families; it is not a proof of arbitrary-program equivalence.
records.provenance: Deterministically generated C-to-Rust/C-to-Go safe integer ports with declared operating ranges.
records.real_code_evidence: A seeded sample is labeled equivalent by real clang/UBSan plus rustc/go when the toolchains are available.
records.title: Verified-equivalent negative corpus

records.construction: Symbolic witnesses are attached to frozen source hashes, safe controls, maintenance snapshots, and reproduction bundles.
limitations: Findings are not cited as upstream CVEs or confirmed repository defects until a separate source audit confirms the same pattern upstream.
records.provenance: Actively maintained public port families with maintainer-ready extraction-unit findings and explicit upstream-claim boundaries.
records.real_code_evidence: When clang/UBSan and rustc are available, every finding witness traps on C and is defined on the Rust target while the safe control stays silent.
records.title: Evidence-tiered responsible divergence findings

scope: Included records are persistent corpora, generated corpus manifests, and corpus-backed benchmark populations.  Pure execution studies that do not own a subject population are referenced as validation commands instead of being double-counted as separate datasets.
